subject: Economics
chapter: Simple Applications of Tools of Demand and Supply

formulas.name: Law of Demand
formulas.formula: As price of a commodity increases, the quantity demanded of it decreases, ceteris paribus.

formulas.name: Law of Supply
formulas.formula: As price of a commodity increases, the quantity supplied of it increases, ceteris paribus.

formulas.name: Law of Equilibrium
formulas.formula: At the equilibrium price, the quantity demanded equals the quantity supplied.

formulas.name: Price Ceiling
formulas.formula: A maximum price above which a commodity cannot be sold.
formulas.notes: Used in case of price control

formulas.name: Price Floor
formulas.formula: A minimum price below which a commodity cannot be sold.
formulas.notes: Used in case of price support

formulas.name: Shortage
formulas.formula: Quantity demanded is greater than quantity supplied at a given price.

formulas.name: Surplus
formulas.formula: Quantity supplied is greater than quantity demanded at a given price.

formulas.name: Market Equilibrium
formulas.formula: A situation where the quantity demanded equals the quantity supplied at a given price.
formulas.notes: The market tends to move towards equilibrium

formulas.name: Supply Curve
formulas.formula: A graphical representation of the relationship between price and quantity supplied.
formulas.notes: Upward sloping as price increases quantity supplied

formulas.name: Demand Curve
formulas.formula: A graphical representation of the relationship between price and quantity demanded.
formulas.notes: Downward sloping as price increases quantity demanded
